rag_rules: Fix type errors by ensuring AGREE expressions resolve to valid bool, int, or real types using only visible identifiers from the current scope.
When AGREE diagnostics report '<error>' types or 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and correct the root unresolved reference or delete the invalid clause.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use only identifiers and types visible in the current AADL component scope.
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and avoid introducing undefined or package-qualified references.
Do not introduce new architecture or model dependencies during repair; only fix existing AGREE syntax and type issues.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE binary expressions '=>' require both sides to be of type bool.
Helper functions and constants must be declared with matching scalar types: int, real, or bool.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to resolve type mismatches by inventing new declarations; instead, correct or remove the problematic clause.
irrelevant_topics: Component type vs implementation scope distinctions
